data.title: Job cuts climb 39% in August, boosting YTD total higher than all of 2024
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 85,979 job cuts in August, up 39% from July and 13% higher than August 2024, Challenger, Gray & Christmas said on Thursday.

So far in 2025, companies have announced 892,362 job cuts, the highest year-to-date total since 2020, when the pandemic led to 1.96M announced job cuts as entire industry sectors, such as leisure and hospitality, ground nearly to a halt.

The YTD total climbed 66% from the same period last year, and has already exceeded full-year 2024’s total by 17%.

“After the impact of DOGE on the federal government, employers are citing economic and market factors as the driver of layoffs,” said Andrew Challenger, senior vice president at Challenger, Gray & Christmas. “We’ve also seen a spike in cuts due to operation or store closings and bankruptcies this year compared to last.” 

As for hiring plans announced so far this year, employers plan to add 87,626 jobs, up 10% from the first eight months of 2024. In August, hiring plans totaled 1,494 jobs, the lowest monthly total for the month since Challenger started tracking hiring plans in 2009.

Among industry sectors, the pharmaceutical industry announced the most job cuts in August at 19,112, surging from 1,359 in July and 1,561 in August 2024. Year-to-date, the industry announced 22,433 job cuts, up 142% from the same period a year ago. 

Challenger attributes the industry’s payrolls slashing to increased competition as patents expire and refocusing on higher-profit products. Many of the companies are also applying AI, causing restructuring and leading to job loss.

The financial industrial industry announced 18,092 in job cuts, bringing the year-to-date total to 44,986, up 27% Y/Y.

Technology reported plans for 12,988 job cuts. That totals 102,239 for the year-to-date, down 3% from the same period in 2024.

Retailers’ announced job cuts for the first eight months of 2025 climbed 242% Y/Y to 83,656. That sector has been hit by tariffs, inflation, and ongoing economic uncertainty. For the month, retail job cuts were 3,169, up from 622 in July and 1,412 in August 2024.

Non-profits announced 4,325 job cuts for the month, resulting in 22,151 YTD, up more than fivefold from 4,032 in the same period last year.

More on the US Labor Market

  • July JOLTS: More Evidence That The Labor Market Weakened This Summer

  • Fed's Waller sees 'multiple' rate cuts in months ahead; sees tariffs slowing growth

  • Job openings drop in July in JOLTS report, signaling softer labor market

  • Payrolls annual benchmark revision expected to point to more subdued job creation

data.title: Burberry jumps back onto the FTSE 100 Index as the turnaround takes hold
data.description:
Burberry Group plc (OTCPK:BURBY) (OTCPK:BBRYF) has been added back to the FTSE 100 Index after an absence of one year. The index inclusion will take effect at the start of trading on September 22.

The FTSE Index reshuffle is part of a scheduled quarterly review by FTSE Russell, aiming to reflect current market capitalizations within the index.

Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) lost its coveted FTSE 100 spot in 2024 but has since undergone a significant transformation, led by CEO Joshua Schulman.

"Over the past year, we have moved from stabilising the business to driving Burberry Forward with confidence. The improvement in our first quarter comparable sales, strength in our core categories, and uptick in brand desirability gives us conviction in the path ahead," highlighted Schulman in the company's last trading update. "Our Autumn 2025 collection is being well received by a broad range of luxury customers as it arrives in stores. Although the external environment remains challenging and we are still in the early stages of our transformation, we are encouraged by the initial progress we are starting to see," he added.

Shares of Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) are up 29% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Spire Global rises on securing $11.1 million NOAA contract for satellite weather data
data.description:
Spire Global (NYSE:SPIR) was awarded an $11,190,900 contract from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to provide global navigation satellite system radio occultation (RO) data for one year from September 18, 2025, to September 18, 2026.

The company's near-real-time GNSS-RO data consists of vertical profiles of atmospheric measurements, including pressure, humidity, and temperature, that can reach all points of the globe.

NOAA, together with NASA, the U.S. Air Force, and the U.S. Navy, will integrate Spire’s GNSS-RO data into their weather and space weather models, while additional U.S. and international agencies will leverage the data to strengthen forecasts and advance climate research.

The award is part of an Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract for NOAA’s Commercial Weather Data Program Radio Occultation Data Buy II.

Spire Global (NYSE:SPIR) +2.6% premarket.

data.title: JetBlue says booking demand was strong throughout the summer peak
data.description:
JetBlue Airways Corporation (NASDAQ:JBLU) trickled lower in early trading on Thursday after the airline company issued an operational and financial update.

The carrier noted that demand for air travel remained strong throughout the summer peak. Momentum from earlier in the summer carried forward into August and through the Labor Day holiday, both of which were marked by strength for bookings within 14 days of travel. JetBlue (NASDAQ:JBLU) said that as it continues to focus on improving reliability, strong operational performance in August contributed to better-than-expected revenue performance. While it is early in the booking curve, JetBlue (JBLU) remains encouraged that current trends may carry forward through the end of the year.

JetBlue (JBLU) also said its non-fuel unit costs benefitted from strong August operational performance and continued execution on cost initiatives. Additionally, it was highlighted that fuel prices have declined since the previous guidance update, which is also helping lead to improved projected operating expenses.

For Q3, JetBlue (JBLU) sees capacity growth of 0% to 1% vs. a prior outlook of -1% to -2%. Operating revenue per available seat mile is expected to be -4.0% to -1.5% vs. -6.0% to -2.0% prior guidance.

Shares of JetBlue (JBLU) were down 0.7% in premarket trading to $5.41 vs. the 52-week range of $3.34 to $8.31.

data.title: Ingevity to sell CTO refinery, most of industrial specialties product line in $110M deal
data.description:
Ingevity (NGTV) said pre-market Thursday that it agreed to sell its North Charleston crude tall oil refinery assets and the majority of its Performance Chemicals Industrial Specialties product line to Mainstream Pine Products for ~$110M in cash plus a potential contingent payment of as much as $19M.

The two companies will enter into a series of agreements related to the transaction, including a supply agreement where Mainstream will supply certain refinery products to support Ingevity's (NYSE:NGVT) Road Technologies product line, and the companies will enter into a toll-manufacturing agreement where Ingevity will utilize its non-refinery assets in North Charleston, South Carolina, to derivatize certain Industrial Specialties products for Mainstream.

Aside from the impact of classifying the combined assets being sold as discontinued operations, Ingevity (NYSE:NGVT) affirmed full-year guidance of revenues of $1.25B-$1.4B and adjusted EBITDA of $390M-$415M.

data.title: IonQ appoints Inder Singh as CFO and COO
data.description:
Quantum computing and networking company IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) announced on Thursday the appointment of Inder Singh as CFO and COO, effective immediately.

Singh succeeds Thomas Kramer, who is set to remain with the company in an advisory capacity for up to 60 days.

Singh has stepped down from the board.

Most recently, he served as CFO of Arm, a British semiconductor and software design company.

data.title: Palantir partners with Lumen, expands Lear Corp. pact
data.description:
• Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) and Lumen Technologies (NYSE:LUMN) are  partnering to bring Palantir’s Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform to Lumen as it shifts from a traditional telecom to a next-gen tech infrastructure company.
  • The tools will help Lumen streamline operations, improve decision-making, and modernize systems. The collaboration is designed to boost efficiency and speed Lumen’s transformation into an AI-ready, cloud-powered network provider.
  • LUMN +6.7% premarket to $5.04.
  • Separately, Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) announced a five-year expansion of its partnership with Lear Corp. (NYSE:LEA), as part of which Lear will broaden its use of Palantir Foundry, as well as Palantir’s Warp Speed manufacturing operating system and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) across its global manufacturing footprint.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aspira Women’s Health appoints Brian Hungerford as new CFO
